skill.name: developer-growth-analysis
skill.description: Analyzes recent Claude Code chat history to identify development patterns, skill gaps, and learning resources, then delivers a growth report to Slack.
skill.summary: Create a personalized developer growth report from recent Claude Code chats.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static external-command and weak-cryptography alerts were reviewed and found to be false positives from markdown examples, inline backticks, and ordinary prose. The confirmed risk is privacy exposure because the skill reads local Claude Code chat history, may process pasted content, searches external resources, and sends a generated report to Slack.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Claude Code Chat History Access
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to read ~/.claude/history.jsonl and process user messages, project names, timestamps, and pasted content. This is useful for the stated purpose, but it can expose private code, credentials, or project details if the report is mishandled.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file path and fields are explicitly documented, including pastedContents. This confirms local hidden-file access to potentially sensitive developer history.

medium_findings.title: External Slack Delivery of Derived Work History
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to send the complete growth report to the user through Slack DMs. The report may contain summaries of private projects, technologies, challenges, and recommendations derived from local chat history.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Slack delivery workflow is explicitly described and includes sending the complete report. The exact data sent depends on report content, so the risk is privacy exposure rather than confirmed malicious exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: External Search Queries Based on Private Work Patterns
medium_findings.description: The skill uses Rube MCP to search HackerNews for resources related to identified improvement areas. Search terms may reveal technologies, problem areas, or project context inferred from private chat history.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly construct external search queries from improvement areas. The exposure is indirect but plausible because those areas are derived from private chats.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Command Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The Ruby shell backtick detections correspond to markdown fences, inline field names, or example report formatting. No evidence found of executable shell commands or command injection instructions in the reviewed lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are visible markdown examples or documentation. They do not instruct Ruby execution or any shell command invocation.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts do not correspond to cryptographic code or instructions. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak algorithms in the reviewed content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain description text, report examples, or learning-resource examples. None contain cryptographic operations.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alert Is a Contextual False Positive
low_findings.description: The flagged line asks the assistant to identify challenges in chat history. It does not request host profiling, environment enumeration, process listing, or system discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding section is about learning analysis from user conversations. The intent is developer coaching, not system reconnaissance.

dangerous_patterns.title: Reads Hidden Local Claude History File
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill accesses a hidden file in the user home directory that may contain sensitive chat history and pasted code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hidden file path and sensitive fields are explicitly named.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sends Generated Report to an External Slack Service
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs use of Rube MCP Slack tools to deliver the complete report through Slack DMs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external delivery path is explicit. The privacy risk depends on the report contents.

content.user_title: Analyze Developer Growth from Claude Chats
content.value_statement: Developers often miss patterns in their recent coding work. This skill reviews Claude Code chat history and creates a focused growth report with learning resources.
actual_capabilities: Reads recent Claude Code chat history from the local history file.
Identifies projects, technologies, problem types, and recurring challenges.
Prioritizes three to five evidence-based improvement areas.
Creates a structured developer growth report with strengths and action items.
Searches HackerNews for learning resources related to improvement areas.
Sends the completed report to the user through Slack DMs.
limitations: Requires access to local Claude Code chat history.
May miss work that happened outside Claude Code conversations.
Resource quality depends on available HackerNews search results.
Slack delivery requires a working Rube MCP Slack connection.

use_cases.title: Weekly Growth Review
use_cases.description: Turn recent coding conversations into a focused weekly learning plan.

use_cases.title: Mentorship Preparation
use_cases.description: Create evidence-based notes before a mentoring or manager discussion.

use_cases.title: Team Learning Signals
use_cases.description: Spot recurring technical themes that can inform team learning topics.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Growth Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a recent work review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my developer growth from my recent Claude Code chats.

prompt_templates.title: Today Focus
prompt_templates.scenario: Review only the current day of work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my work from today and suggest three areas for improvement.

prompt_templates.title: Targeted Skill Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Focus on one technology or problem area.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my recent Claude Code chats and focus on my TypeScript, testing, and architecture patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Learning Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a prioritized plan with external resources and Slack delivery.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the last 48 hours of my Claude Code chats, identify my top growth areas, find relevant HackerNews resources, and send the report to my Slack DMs.

output_examples.input: Analyze my developer growth from my recent chats.
output_examples.output: A report that summarizes recent projects, identifies top improvement areas, lists observed strengths, and recommends action items.

output_examples.input: Analyze my work from today and suggest areas for improvement.
output_examples.output: A same-day review with prioritized learning topics, supporting evidence from chat patterns, and suggested next steps.

output_examples.input: Find learning resources for the gaps you identify.
output_examples.output: A curated list of HackerNews articles and discussions mapped to each improvement area.

best_practices: Review the report before sharing it outside your private workspace.
Run the analysis on a regular schedule, such as once per week.
Focus on one improvement area before moving to the next.
anti_patterns: Do not use this on chats that contain secrets or customer data.
Do not treat the report as a complete performance review.
Do not send reports to Slack channels without explicit consent.

faq.question: What data does this skill analyze?
faq.answer: It analyzes recent Claude Code chat history, including messages, project names, timestamps, and pasted content.

faq.question: Does it modify my code?
faq.answer: No. It reviews chat history and creates a learning report.

faq.question: Can it work without Slack?
faq.answer: The report can be presented in the assistant response, but Slack delivery requires a connected Slack account.

faq.question: Why does it use HackerNews?
faq.answer: It searches HackerNews for articles and discussions related to the improvement areas it finds.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive projects?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill may process private code or project details from chat history.

faq.question: How often should I run it?
faq.answer: Weekly use gives enough recent work to find patterns without making the report too broad.
